title: Way of the Wookiee!

[[2 BBY/Legends|2]]–[[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]<ref name="Way of the Wookiee date">The events of ''[[Way of the Wookiee!]]'' are alluded to in the novel ''[[Rebel Dawn]]'' during [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo's]] last [[Kessel Run/Legends|Kessel Run]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. ''The New Essential Chronology'' also dates Solo's acquisition of the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], which is featured in the comic strip, to [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]. ''Way of the Wookiee'' is therefore set between those two dates.</ref>

"'''Way of the Wookiee!'''" was a story published in 1979 in three parts in Marvel UK's ''[[Star Wars Weekly 94]]–[[Star Wars Weekly 96|96]]'', and written by [[Archie Goodwin]]. It was reprinted in 1981 in [[Star Wars (Marvel Illustrated Books)|Marvel Illustrated Books' ''Star Wars'']]. It was named after the first part of the comic, originally published in [[Star Wars Weekly 94|''Star Wars Weekly'' 94]]. The other two original parts are "[[Chewbacca Fights Alone!]]" and "[[Switch!]]"

''Way of the Wookiee!'' tells the story of a botched [[Kessel Run/Legends|Kessel Run]] of [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo's]] (which preceded the one that landed him in trouble with [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]]).

==Plot summary==
Not long before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] travel to the planet [[Formos/Legends|Formos]] to pick up a load of [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]] [[Spice/Legends|spice]] from [[N'Mrith]], a contact of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]]. Before making planetfall, they have a run-in with an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial prison barge|prison ship]] commanded by [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Quirt]], who suspects them as smugglers. Although Han successfully talks his way out of trouble, Quirt remains suspicious, eager to meet his quota of new prisoners for the [[Spice mines of Kessel/Legends|Spice Mines of Kessel]].

Han and Chewie flirt again with arrest when Chewbacca spots a fellow [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] named [[Hronk]], a captive of a group of Quirt's [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], and goes berserk. Han is forced to [[Stun/Legends|stun blast]] his partner, and only another bit of quick thinking saves them from arrest. Chewbacca subsequently explains that his grudge against Hronk stems from an old family feud on their homeworld of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. Han suggests simply allowing the Imperials to take Hronk to the spice mines, but Chewbacca is not satisfied.

Chewbacca subsequently sneaks aboard the Imperial prison ship to rescue Hronk, inciting a riot among the prisoners. Fleeting back to the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] a few steps ahead of Quirt's troops, Chewbacca, Hronk, and Han make a hasty getaway with their newly loaded cargo of spice.

N'Mrith betrays Han and Chewbacca by informing Quirt that the ''Falcon'' is carrying both a cargo of illegal spice and the escaped prisoner. By jettisoning their cargo and disguising Hronk as Chewbacca, they manage to fool Quirt and make their getaway. Quirt announces irritably that he intends to make N'Mrith replace his missing prisoner, allowing him to still meet his quota.

Han and Chewbacca release Hronk—who is decidedly irritable at now being in his enemy's debt—in a nearby system. Unfortunately, the partners realize that they will have to inform Jabba that they have lost the spice they were supposed to deliver.

==Continuity==
This story was originally intended to describe the failed [[Kessel Run/Legends|Kessel Run]] that led to Han Solo's debt to Jabba the Hutt. [[A. C. Crispin]]'s ''[[Rebel Dawn]]'', the third book of [[The Han Solo Trilogy (hardcover)|The Han Solo Trilogy]], alludes to the events of this story, but implies that the price on Han's head stemmed from a subsequent Kessel Run.

"Jabba the Hutt" is spelled "Jabba the Hut" throughout this story, as in all other pre–[[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] ''Star Wars'' works. [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] is misspelled "Kazhyyk."

At different points in this story, Quirt's rank is alternatively [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] (a [[Imperial Navy/Legends|naval]] rank) and [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] (an [[Imperial Army/Legends|army]] rank). When Han mentions these events in ''[[Rebel Dawn]]'', he refers to "Colonel Quirt."

The prison ship that Han Solo had a run-in with in the story resembles the ''[[Doomgiver]]'' used by the [[Imperial Remnant]].
